subject: Product Management
title: The IBM PC
description: A quick, practical guide to The IBM PC.

hook.text: IBM built its personal computer in 12 months with off-the-shelf parts. One shortcut created a trillion-dollar industry.

buildup.text: IBM was a mainframe giant. When PCs emerged, executives dismissed them — until Apple started winning business customers.

discovery.text: To ship fast, IBM used an open architecture with third-party components. Anyone could build compatible hardware.

twist.text: IBM outsourced the operating system to a tiny company called Microsoft — and didn't secure exclusive rights.

climax.text: That one product decision let Microsoft license DOS to every PC maker. IBM lost the war it started.

punchline.text: IBM built the PC market. Microsoft and Intel kept the profits.

quiz.question: What product decision cost IBM its dominance in the PC market?
options: Using open architecture and not securing exclusive OS rights from Microsoft
Making the PC too expensive for consumers
Refusing to enter the personal computer market
